title: Specify codex rule frontmatter
description: Validates the YAML frontmatter block at the top of a codex rule markdown file. The schema applies only to the machine-readable frontmatter between the opening and closing `---` delimiters; Markdown body validation, including the required `## Rule` heading and duplicate-id checks across the first-party rule set, is handled by `specdev check`.

id.description: Stable codex rule identifier. V1 reserves UNI, SRC, FRAME, RUST, IFACE, SEC, OMNIA, VECTIS, and ORG namespaces with three-digit numeric suffixes. SRC namespaces shared source-adapter overlays; FRAME is reserved for RFC-32 framework declarative checks and is not used in consumer adapter codex.

severity.description: Default review severity for findings associated with this rule.

trigger.description: One-sentence condition that tells a generator or reviewer when the rule matters.

applicability.description: Small V1 applicability filter. These fields narrow where a rule is likely relevant; they are not suppressions, exceptions, or policy overrides.

languages.description: Lowercase language or format tokens such as `rust`, `typescript`, or `json-schema`.

artifacts.description: Lowercase artifact category tokens such as `code`, `tests`, `contracts`, or `specs`.

paths.description: Relative path or glob patterns. Absolute paths, URI strings, and `..` segments are rejected.

review_mode.description: How this rule is expected to be reviewed. The review runner decides execution; this field only classifies the rule.

deterministic_hints.description: Optional hints for future deterministic checks. V1 validates only the hint shape, not regex syntax or tool availability.

kind.description: Kind of deterministic signal the hint describes. Phase 2 kinds (`path-pattern`, `regex`, `schema`, `tool`) are executed by the `specrun review` hint interpreter; reserved kinds carry `x-rfc32-status: reserved` and are accepted by RFC-28 exporters and tooling validators per RFC-32 §Hint kinds — reserved, but are shape-validated only at this layer with no execution semantics implied.

value.description: Hint payload, interpreted by a future validator or review tool.

description.description: Optional human explanation for the hint.

references.description: External or repository-local reference material that helps apply the rule.

label.description: Short display label for the reference.

deprecated.description: When present, marks this rule as deprecated while preserving the stable id for historical citations.

reason.description: Human-readable reason the rule is deprecated.

replaced_by.description: Replacement codex rule id, when there is a direct successor.
